scope_boundary: Partitioned-authority fixture only. The fixture validates three finite records and rejects six expected-invalid controls for stale-grant dispatch, mutation after unseen revocation, grant/effect race residual ownership, missing no-mutation evidence, support-state promotion, and missing non-claim boundaries. It does not run a distributed authority service, network partition test, deployed hive scheduler, runtime adapter enforcement service, consensus system, availability test, revocation propagation service, benchmark, security audit, or privacy audit.
transition_reason: The partitioned-authority fixture is useful because it makes stale grants, revocation-delay quarantine, fresh authority receipts, grant/effect race residual ownership, no-mutation evidence, CAP-style authority consistency limits, support-state boundaries, and non-claims executable as finite hives/runtime records. It remains a synthetic record fixture, not deployed distributed-authority evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed partition tolerance, distributed consensus, availability, runtime-adapter enforcement, revocation propagation, hive scheduler behavior, rented-node sandbox behavior, family-governance behavior, network-overlay behavior, security, privacy, benchmark performance, or chapter-core support.
required_artifacts: live or externally reviewable grant/revocation propagation trace
fresh-authority receipt trace from a deployed or externally reviewable authority service
runtime adapter enforcement trace under stale-grant pressure
hive scheduler or worker trace under partition pressure
security and privacy review for cross-device authority handling
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: mutation after unseen revocation is rejected
grant/effect race without residual owner is rejected
partition denial without no-mutation evidence is rejected
support-state promotion from fixture shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: mutation after unseen revocation accepted without quarantine or no-mutation evidence
grant/effect race accepted without residual ownership
support-state promotion accepted from fixture shape

fixture described as deployed partition-tolerance, consensus, availability, revocation-propagation, runtime-adapter enforcement, hive-scheduler, security, privacy, benchmark, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed partitioned-authority claim after live or externally reviewable grant/revocation propagation traces, partition/reconciliation replay, fresh-authority receipt behavior, runtime-adapter enforcement under stale-grant pressure, distributed authority-service behavior, hive scheduler or worker behavior under partition pressure, residual accounting, security and privacy review, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: no distributed consensus or availability result
does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent distributed-systems, runtime-adapter, hive-scheduler, authority-service, security, privacy, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no live or externally reviewable grant/revocation propagation trace
no runtime-adapter enforcement trace under stale-grant pressure
no deployed hive scheduler or worker trace under partition pressure
non_claims: does not promote partitioned-authority evidence above argument

does not prove deployed partition tolerance, distributed consensus, availability, runtime-adapter enforcement, revocation propagation, hive scheduler behavior, rented-node sandbox behavior, family-governance behavior, network-overlay behavior, security, privacy, model quality, benchmark performance, safety, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Personal Compute Hives chapter core claim
does not promote the Runtime Adapters chapter core claim
